Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-05-169
Dear Mr. Olson:
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the
campaignreporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I iour letter
requested informal assistance on behalf of one of your clients, which you only identified
as "a non-profit public benefit.corporation." After careful consideration, we have
decided to decline your request for informal assistance.
The Commission provides formal written advice and informal assistance with
respect to the duties and responsibilities that persons have under the Act. Commission
regulation 18329 (copy enclosed) describes the manner in which this advice and informal
assistance is provided. Subdivision (b) of the regulation declares that the Commission
will not provide formal written advice unless the name, title or position, and mailing
address of the person whose duties are in question are providea. fne Commissio, *uy
decline to provide informal assistance for the same reason. (Reg. lg329,subd. (c)(4)G).)
Lacking such information, the Commission is placed in the potential position ofiuAging '
past conduct or conductthat is currently the subject of an enforcement action Uylfris oi
some other agency. To do so would be contrary to public policy.
While we should have notified you immediatel y thatwe would have to decline
your request for informal assistance due to the anonymity of your client, we regrettably
failed to do so for a significant period of time because of the press of other business and a
failure to adequately screen your request early on to deterrnine if we would be able to
I The Act is contained in Government Code sections 81000 throughglll4. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political
Practices commission are contained in sections 18109 through 18997 of title 2 of the califomia code of
Regulations. All regulatory references are to title 2, division 6 of the California Code of Regulations,
unless otherwise indicated.


I
FileNo. W-05-169
Page No. 2
provide the assistance that you sought. We apolo gize for any inconvenience that this
mayhave caused for you or your client.
Nonetheless, staff subsequently contacted you by telephone to request that you
disclose the name of your client; so that we would be able to provide the assistance that
you sought. Since this information has not been provided, we are declining your request
for informal assistance at this time.
If you remain interested in receiving either advice or informal assistance from the
Commission on behalf of your client concerning the questions that you posed, please
submit another request that identifies your client in accordance with regulation 18329.
Should you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(e16) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: A non-profit public benefit corporation sought informal assistance regarding campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the client was not identified by name.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide informal assistance because the requestor failed to identify the client by name, title/position, and mailing address as required by regulation 18329, and the Commission cannot provide advice regarding potentially anonymous or unidentifiable parties.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (Form W-05-169). No Q/C sections present; structure is introductory letter format. Clear explanation of why assistance was declined (anonymity of client) and how to resubmit compliant request provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A non-profit public benefit corporation sought informal assistance regarding campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the client was not identified by name.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide informal assistance because the requestor failed to identify the client by name, title/position, and mailing address as required by regulation 18329, and the Commission cannot provide advice regarding potentially anonymous or unidentifiable parties.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines an informal assistance request from attorney Lance Olson on behalf of an unidentified non-profit public benefit corporation regarding campaign reporting provisions, citing violation of regulation 18329 requirements for client identification.
